attachment.content:
Entire CLI will link this conversation to your next commit.
  1 other active conversation(s) in this workspace will also be included.
  Use 'entire status' for more information.

- update-config: Use this skill to configure the Claude Code harness via settings.json. Automated behaviors ("from now on when X", "each time X", "whenever X", "before/after X") require hooks configured in settings.json - the harness executes these, not Claude, so memory/preferences cannot fulfill them. Also use for: permissions ("allow X", "add permission", "move permission to"), env vars ("set X=Y"), hook troubleshooting, or any changes to settings.json/settings.local.json files. Examples: "allow npm commands", "add bq permission to global settings", "move permission to user settings", "set DEBUG=true", "when claude stops show X". For simple settings like theme/model, suggest the /config command.
- keybindings-help: Use when the user wants to customize keyboard shortcuts, rebind keys, add chord bindings, or modify ~/.claude/keybindings.json. Examples: "rebind ctrl+s", "add a chord shortcut", "change the submit key", "customize keybindings".
- simplify: Review changed code for reuse, quality, and efficiency, then fix any issues found.
- fewer-permission-prompts: Scan your transcripts for common read-only Bash and MCP tool calls, then add a prioritized allowlist to project .claude/settings.json to reduce permission prompts.
- loop: Run a prompt or slash command on a recurring interval (e.g. /loop 5m /foo). Omit the interval to let the model self-pace. - When the user wants to set up a recurring task, poll for status, or run something repeatedly on an interval (e.g. "check the deploy every 5 minutes", "keep running /babysit-prs"). Do NOT invoke for one-off tasks.
- schedule: Create, update, list, or run scheduled remote agents (routines) that execute on a cron schedule. - When the user wants to schedule a recurring remote agent, set up automated tasks, create a cron job for Claude Code, or manage their scheduled agents/routines. Also use when the user wants a one-time scheduled run ("run this once at 3pm", "remind me to check X tomorrow").
- claude-api: Build, debug, and optimize Claude API / Anthropic SDK apps. Apps built with this skill should include prompt caching. Also handles migrating existing Claude API code between Claude model versions (4.5 → 4.6, 4.6 → 4.7, retired-model replacements).
TRIGGER when: code imports `anthropic`/`@anthropic-ai/sdk`; user asks for the Claude API, Anthropic SDK, or Managed Agents; user adds/modifies/tunes a Claude feature (caching, thinking, compaction, tool use, batch, files, citations, memory) or model (Opus/Sonnet/Haiku) in a file; questions about prompt caching / cache hit rate in an Anthropic SDK project.
SKIP: file imports `openai`/other-provider SDK, filename like `*-openai.py`/`*-generic.py`, provider-neutral code, general programming/ML.
- init: Initialize a new CLAUDE.md file with codebase documentation
- review: Review a pull request
- security-review: Complete a security review of the pending changes on the current branch

1	# WP-023 — CHR-V3-03: Status Bar with Lens Dropdown
2	
3	**Branch:** `feat/v3-chr-v3-03-status-bar`
4	**Effort:** S
5	**State:** `now`
6	
7	---
8	
9	## Required reading
10	
11	Read every file below end-to-end before writing a single line of code.
12	
13	- `/Users/jhoetter/repos/bim-ai/spec/workpackage-tracker-v3.md` — §2 CHR-V3-03 entry (line 334); also read CHR-V3-04 (line 344) for context on the 6-slot status bar layout
14	- `/Users/jhoetter/repos/bim-ai/CLAUDE.md`
15	- `/Users/jhoetter/repos/bim-ai/packages/web/src/` — locate the existing status bar component (search for `StatusBar`, `status-bar`, or `statusBar`); read its full implementation
16	- `/Users/jhoetter/repos/bim-ai/packages/web/src/` — locate the lens/discipline store (search for `LensMode`, `lensMode`, `DSC-V3-02`, `defaultLens`); read the lens dispatch logic
17	- `/Users/jhoetter/repos/bim-ai/packages/core/src/index.ts` — `LensMode` type and discipline-related types
18	- `/Users/jhoetter/repos/bim-ai/packages/web/src/` — locate `monitorDriftBadge` aggregate or equivalent drift-count selector
19	
20	---
21	
22	## Verbatim CHR-V3-03 tracker entry (§2, line 334)
23	
24	**CHR-V3-03 — Status bar with lens dropdown**
25	
26	**Status.** `now`. T8 LNS-V3-01 home.
27	**Scope.** 6-slot status bar; lens dropdown is the keystone (A8 antidote). 28 px, `--color-surface-strong`, `--radius-canvas: 0`.
28	**Data model.** Reuses T8 DSC-V3-02 `defaultLens: LensMode`.
29	**Engine.** Lens dispatch reuses T8 commands. Drift count from `monitorDriftBadge` aggregate.
30	**UI.** `--text-2xs` slots; lens menu `--text-sm`. `--ease-paper` 240 ms. Drift in `--color-drift`.
31	**Acceptance.** "Show: All" → "Show: Structure" ghosts arch to 25 % in 240 ms. No filter dialog. Drift opens federation panel.
32	**Effort.** S.
33	
34	---
35	
36	## Setup
37	
38	```bash
39	git fetch origin && git checkout main && git pull --ff-only
40	git checkout -b feat/v3-chr-v3-03-status-bar
41	cd /Users/jhoetter/repos/bim-ai
42	
43	# Locate the existing status bar component
44	grep -rn "StatusBar\|status.bar\|status-bar" packages/web/src/ --include="*.tsx" --include="*.ts" -l
45	
46	# Locate lens/discipline store
47	grep -rn "LensMode\|lensMode\|defaultLens" packages/web/src/ --include="*.tsx" --include="*.ts" -l
48	
49	# Locate drift badge
50	grep -rn "monitorDriftBadge\|driftBadge\|driftCount" packages/web/src/ --include="*.tsx" --include="*.ts" -l
51	
52	pnpm install
53	```
54	
55	---
56	
57	## Implementation tasks
58	
59	### 1. `LensMode` type — `packages/core/src/index.ts`
60	
61	Confirm `LensMode` is already exported (added by T8 DSC-V3-02). If not, add it:
62	
63	```ts
64	export type LensMode =
65	  | 'all'
66	  | 'architecture'
67	  | 'structure'
68	  | 'mep'
69	  | 'energy'
70	  | 'coordination';
71	```
72	
73	If it exists under a different name (e.g. `DisciplineMode`), add `LensMode` as an alias or re-export. Do not rename the existing type — add an alias.
74	
75	### 2. Status bar layout — 6 slots
76	
77	The status bar is a 28 px high, full-width bar at the bottom of the canvas. It has 6 named slots:
78	
79	- Slot 1 (left): model name / save status
80	- Slot 2: active level / cut-plane indicator
81	- Slot 3: view type label (Plan | Section | 3D | Sheet)
82	- Slot 4: active phase
83	- Slot 5 (keystone): lens dropdown — "Show: All" / "Show: Structure" / etc.
84	- Slot 6 (right): drift badge (hidden when drift = 0)
85	
86	If a status bar component already exists in the codebase, extend it to implement this slot layout. If none exists, create `packages/web/src/chrome/StatusBar.tsx`.
87	
88	The bar must be styled with:
89	```css
90	height: 28px;
91	background: var(--color-surface-strong);
92	border-radius: var(--radius-canvas);   /* = 0 */
93	font-size: var(--text-2xs);
94	```
95	
96	### 3. Lens dropdown (slot 5)
97	
98	The lens dropdown is the A8 antidote — it replaces the old filter dialog with a single in-bar control:
99	
100	```tsx
101	// LensDropdown.tsx — part of StatusBar or a separate file in chrome/
102	interface LensDropdownProps {
103	  currentLens: LensMode;
104	  onLensChange: (lens: LensMode) => void;
105	}
106	
107	export function LensDropdown({ currentLens, onLensChange }: LensDropdownProps) {
108	  // Renders a dropdown trigger "Show: All" (or current lens label)
109	  // Opens a menu with lens options
110	  // Menu uses --text-sm font size
111	  // Transition uses --ease-paper 240 ms
112	  // Selecting a new lens:
113	  //   1. Dispatches to the T8 lens store (reuses existing T8 command)
114	  //   2. Updates UI optimistically
115	}
116	```
117	
118	The label format is `"Show: {lens}"` where lens is title-cased (e.g. `"Show: All"`, `"Show: Structure"`, `"Show: MEP"`).
119	
120	When the lens changes from `All` to `Structure`, non-structural elements ghost to 25 % opacity. This opacity change must complete in 240 ms using `--ease-paper`. Implement the ghost by:
121	- Dispatching the T8 lens command (which updates the lens store).
122	- The canvas/renderer subscribes to the lens store and applies the opacity — this WP wires the dispatch; the renderer integration is a follow-on if the renderer does not yet support it. Add a `// TODO(T8/renderer): apply lens ghost opacity` comment if the renderer side is not yet implemented.
123	
124	### 4. Drift badge (slot 6)
125	
126	The drift badge shows a count of federation drifts from `monitorDriftBadge`:
127	
128	```tsx
129	// DriftBadge.tsx — part of StatusBar or a separate file in chrome/
130	interface DriftBadgeProps {
131	  driftCount: number;
132	  onClick: () => void;  // opens the federation panel
133	}
134	
135	export function DriftBadge({ driftCount, onClick }: DriftBadgeProps) {
136	  if (driftCount === 0) return null;
137	  return (
138	    <button
139	      style={{ color: 'var(--color-drift)' }}
140	      onClick={onClick}
141	    >
142	      {driftCount} drift{driftCount !== 1 ? 's' : ''}
143	    </button>
144	  );
145	}
146	```
147	
148	The `--color-drift` token must be present in the design system. If it is not yet defined, add it to the CSS token file (locate via `grep -rn "color-drift\|--drift" packages/web/src/`). Use `oklch(65% 0.18 50)` as the initial value (amber warning tone consistent with the bim-ai token palette).
149	
150	### 5. Keyboard shortcut registration
151	
152	Register a keyboard shortcut that cycles through lens modes when the status bar lens is focused:
153	
154	- `Tab` while lens dropdown is open cycles to next lens.
155	- `Escape` closes the dropdown and restores focus to canvas.
156	
157	These shortcuts follow the existing shortcut registration pattern in the codebase (locate via `grep -rn "registerShortcut\|useKeyboard\|keyboardShortcut" packages/web/src/ -l`).
158	
159	### 6. Tests
160	
161	- `packages/web/src/chrome/StatusBar.test.tsx` (or `.spec.tsx`):
162	  - Renders with 6 slots visible.
163	  - Slot 5 shows `"Show: All"` by default.
164	  - Clicking slot 5 opens the lens menu.
165	  - Selecting `"Structure"` calls `onLensChange("structure")`.
166	  - `DriftBadge` is hidden when `driftCount=0`.
167	  - `DriftBadge` is visible and shows count when `driftCount=3`.
168	  - `DriftBadge` click calls `onClick`.
169	  - No hex literals in the component tree (ESLint `bim-ai/no-hex-in-chrome` enforced).
170	
171	Use the existing test pattern in `packages/web/src/` (Vitest + React Testing Library).
172	
173	### 7. Storybook story (optional but recommended)
174	
175	If Storybook is configured (check `packages/web/` for `.storybook/`), add a story:
176	
177	```tsx
178	// StatusBar.stories.tsx
179	export const Default = { args: { lensMode: 'all', driftCount: 0 } };
180	export const WithDrift = { args: { lensMode: 'all', driftCount: 5 } };
181	export const LensStructure = { args: { lensMode: 'structure', driftCount: 0 } };
182	```
183	
184	---
185	
186	## Implementation rules
187	
188	- **No hex literals in chrome.** Every colour in `StatusBar.tsx`, `LensDropdown.tsx`, and `DriftBadge.tsx` must use `var(--…)` CSS token references. The ESLint `bim-ai/no-hex-in-chrome` rule is active; CI fails on any literal hex in new `.tsx` files. The single exception is the `--color-drift` token initialisation value in the CSS token file itself — token definitions may use `oklch(…)` or `#` values; component code may not.
189	- **28 px height is fixed.** Do not use `min-height` or `height: auto`. The bar must be exactly 28 px.
190	- **`--radius-canvas: 0`.** The bar has no border-radius (sharp edges, flush with the canvas bottom).
191	- **`--ease-paper` for all transitions.** Lens change, drift badge appear/disappear — all use `transition: opacity 240ms var(--ease-paper)`.
192	- **Lens dispatch reuses T8 commands.** Do not invent a new store or command. Import and use whatever T8 DSC-V3-02 shipped for lens state.
193	- **No filter dialog.** The lens dropdown is the complete filter surface. No modal, no sheet overlay. This is the A8 antidote — the whole point is that it replaces the dialog.
194	- **Drift badge opens federation panel.** The onClick for `DriftBadge` must open the federation/links panel. Locate the existing panel toggle action (search `packages/web/src/` for `federationPanel`, `linksPanel`, or `FederationPanel`) and dispatch it.
195	- **Pydantic alias discipline** does not apply (this is a pure frontend WP), but follow the same `camelCase` prop naming convention in TypeScript interfaces.
196	
197	---
198	
199	## Verify gate
200	
201	```bash
202	# TypeScript type-check
203	cd /Users/jhoetter/repos/bim-ai
204	pnpm tsc --noEmit
205	
206	# Unit tests
207	pnpm exec vitest run packages/web/src/chrome/
208	
209	# ESLint (no-hex-in-chrome)
210	pnpm exec eslint packages/web/src/chrome/StatusBar.tsx \
211	                  packages/web/src/chrome/LensDropdown.tsx \
212	                  packages/web/src/chrome/DriftBadge.tsx
213	
214	# Format check
215	pnpm format:check
216	```
217	
218	Manual verification (dev server on :3000 or :5173):
219	- Load a model; status bar appears at 28 px height at canvas bottom.
220	- `"Show: All"` is visible in slot 5.
221	- Click slot 5; lens menu opens.
222	- Select `"Structure"`; canvas ghosts non-structural elements to 25 % in 240 ms.
223	- Select `"All"`; canvas restores in 240 ms.
224	- No filter dialog opens at any point.
225	
226	---
227	
228	## Commit and push
229	
230	```bash
231	git add packages/web/src/chrome/ \
232	        packages/core/src/index.ts
233	# Add packages/web/src/design-tokens/*.css if --color-drift was added
234	git commit -m "$(cat <<'EOF'
235	feat(chr): CHR-V3-03 status bar with lens dropdown (WP-023)
236	
237	6-slot 28 px status bar; slot 5 = LensDropdown (A8 antidote, replaces filter dialog).
238	Lens change dispatches T8 DSC-V3-02 command; ghost opacity 240 ms --ease-paper.
239	DriftBadge slot 6: hidden at 0, opens federation panel on click, --color-drift token.
240	No hex literals; --radius-canvas: 0; --text-2xs slots.
241	
242	Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
243	EOF
244	)"
245	git push -u origin feat/v3-chr-v3-03-status-bar
246	```
247	
248	---
249	
250	## Final report template
251	
252	Paste this back to the orchestrator when done:
253	
254	```
255	WP: WP-023 / CHR-V3-03
256	Branch: feat/v3-chr-v3-03-status-bar
257	Status: done
258	Tests: <N> passed (StatusBar.test.tsx)
259	Files changed:
260	  - packages/web/src/chrome/StatusBar.tsx       (6-slot layout, 28 px)
261	  - packages/web/src/chrome/LensDropdown.tsx    (slot 5 keystone, --text-sm menu)
262	  - packages/web/src/chrome/DriftBadge.tsx      (slot 6, --color-drift)
263	  - packages/core/src/index.ts                  (LensMode type if new)
264	  - packages/web/src/design-tokens/*.css        (--color-drift if new)
265	  - <any additional files>
266	Notes: <renderer ghost-opacity integration deferred if T8 renderer not yet wired; etc.>
267	
268	Tracker: §2 CHR-V3-03 → change status to `done`
269	```
270

# WP-023 — CHR-V3-03: Status Bar with Lens Dropdown

**Branch:** `feat/v3-chr-v3-03-status-bar`
**Effort:** S
**State:** `now`

---

## Required reading

Read every file below end-to-end before writing a single line of code.

- `/Users/jhoetter/repos/bim-ai/spec/workpackage-tracker-v3.md` — §2 CHR-V3-03 entry (line 334); also read CHR-V3-04 (line 344) for context on the 6-slot status bar layout
- `/Users/jhoetter/repos/bim-ai/CLAUDE.md`
- `/Users/jhoetter/repos/bim-ai/packages/web/src/` — locate the existing status bar component (search for `StatusBar`, `status-bar`, or `statusBar`); read its full implementation
- `/Users/jhoetter/repos/bim-ai/packages/web/src/` — locate the lens/discipline store (search for `LensMode`, `lensMode`, `DSC-V3-02`, `defaultLens`); read the lens dispatch logic
- `/Users/jhoetter/repos/bim-ai/packages/core/src/index.ts` — `LensMode` type and discipline-related types
- `/Users/jhoetter/repos/bim-ai/packages/web/src/` — locate `monitorDriftBadge` aggregate or equivalent drift-count selector

---

## Verbatim CHR-V3-03 tracker entry (§2, line 334)

**CHR-V3-03 — Status bar with lens dropdown**

**Status.** `now`. T8 LNS-V3-01 home.
**Scope.** 6-slot status bar; lens dropdown is the keystone (A8 antidote). 28 px, `--color-surface-strong`, `--radius-canvas: 0`.
**Data model.** Reuses T8 DSC-V3-02 `defaultLens: LensMode`.
**Engine.** Lens dispatch reuses T8 commands. Drift count from `monitorDriftBadge` aggregate.
**UI.** `--text-2xs` slots; lens menu `--text-sm`. `--ease-paper` 240 ms. Drift in `--color-drift`.
**Acceptance.** "Show: All" → "Show: Structure" ghosts arch to 25 % in 240 ms. No filter dialog. Drift opens federation panel.
**Effort.** S.

---

## Setup

```bash
git fetch origin && git checkout main && git pull --ff-only
git checkout -b feat/v3-chr-v3-03-status-bar
cd /Users/jhoetter/repos/bim-ai

# Locate the existing status bar component
grep -rn "StatusBar\|status.bar\|status-bar" packages/web/src/ --include="*.tsx" --include="*.ts" -l

# Locate lens/discipline store
grep -rn "LensMode\|lensMode\|defaultLens" packages/web/src/ --include="*.tsx" --include="*.ts" -l

# Locate drift badge
grep -rn "monitorDriftBadge\|driftBadge\|driftCount" packages/web/src/ --include="*.tsx" --include="*.ts" -l

pnpm install
```

---

## Implementation tasks

### 1. `LensMode` type — `packages/core/src/index.ts`

Confirm `LensMode` is already exported (added by T8 DSC-V3-02). If not, add it:

```ts
export type LensMode =
  | 'all'
  | 'architecture'
  | 'structure'
  | 'mep'
  | 'energy'
  | 'coordination';
```

If it exists under a different name (e.g. `DisciplineMode`), add `LensMode` as an alias or re-export. Do not rename the existing type — add an alias.

### 2. Status bar layout — 6 slots

The status bar is a 28 px high, full-width bar at the bottom of the canvas. It has 6 named slots:

- Slot 1 (left): model name / save status
- Slot 2: active level / cut-plane indicator
- Slot 3: view type label (Plan | Section | 3D | Sheet)
- Slot 4: active phase
- Slot 5 (keystone): lens dropdown — "Show: All" / "Show: Structure" / etc.
- Slot 6 (right): drift badge (hidden when drift = 0)

If a status bar component already exists in the codebase, extend it to implement this slot layout. If none exists, create `packages/web/src/chrome/StatusBar.tsx`.

The bar must be styled with:
```css
height: 28px;
background: var(--color-surface-strong);
border-radius: var(--radius-canvas);   /* = 0 */
font-size: var(--text-2xs);
```

### 3. Lens dropdown (slot 5)

The lens dropdown is the A8 antidote — it replaces the old filter dialog with a single in-bar control:

```tsx
// LensDropdown.tsx — part of StatusBar or a separate file in chrome/
interface LensDropdownProps {
  currentLens: LensMode;
  onLensChange: (lens: LensMode) => void;
}

export function LensDropdown({ currentLens, onLensChange }: LensDropdownProps) {
  // Renders a dropdown trigger "Show: All" (or current lens label)
  // Opens a menu with lens options
  // Menu uses --text-sm font size
  // Transition uses --ease-paper 240 ms
  // Selecting a new lens:
  //   1. Dispatches to the T8 lens store (reuses existing T8 command)
  //   2. Updates UI optimistically
}
```

The label format is `"Show: {lens}"` where lens is title-cased (e.g. `"Show: All"`, `"Show: Structure"`, `"Show: MEP"`).

When the lens changes from `All` to `Structure`, non-structural elements ghost to 25 % opacity. This opacity change must complete in 240 ms using `--ease-paper`. Implement the ghost by:
- Dispatching the T8 lens command (which updates the lens store).
- The canvas/renderer subscribes to the lens store and applies the opacity — this WP wires the dispatch; the renderer integration is a follow-on if the renderer does not yet support it. Add a `// TODO(T8/renderer): apply lens ghost opacity` comment if the renderer side is not yet implemented.

### 4. Drift badge (slot 6)

The drift badge shows a count of federation drifts from `monitorDriftBadge`:

```tsx
// DriftBadge.tsx — part of StatusBar or a separate file in chrome/
interface DriftBadgeProps {
  driftCount: number;
  onClick: () => void;  // opens the federation panel
}

export function DriftBadge({ driftCount, onClick }: DriftBadgeProps) {
  if (driftCount === 0) return null;
  return (
    <button
      style={{ color: 'var(--color-drift)' }}
      onClick={onClick}
    >
      {driftCount} drift{driftCount !== 1 ? 's' : ''}
    </button>
  );
}
```

The `--color-drift` token must be present in the design system. If it is not yet defined, add it to the CSS token file (locate via `grep -rn "color-drift\|--drift" packages/web/src/`). Use `oklch(65% 0.18 50)` as the initial value (amber warning tone consistent with the bim-ai token palette).

### 5. Keyboard shortcut registration

Register a keyboard shortcut that cycles through lens modes when the status bar lens is focused:

- `Tab` while lens dropdown is open cycles to next lens.
- `Escape` closes the dropdown and restores focus to canvas.

These shortcuts follow the existing shortcut registration pattern in the codebase (locate via `grep -rn "registerShortcut\|useKeyboard\|keyboardShortcut" packages/web/src/ -l`).

### 6. Tests

- `packages/web/src/chrome/StatusBar.test.tsx` (or `.spec.tsx`):
  - Renders with 6 slots visible.
  - Slot 5 shows `"Show: All"` by default.
  - Clicking slot 5 opens the lens menu.
  - Selecting `"Structure"` calls `onLensChange("structure")`.
  - `DriftBadge` is hidden when `driftCount=0`.
  - `DriftBadge` is visible and shows count when `driftCount=3`.
  - `DriftBadge` click calls `onClick`.
  - No hex literals in the component tree (ESLint `bim-ai/no-hex-in-chrome` enforced).

Use the existing test pattern in `packages/web/src/` (Vitest + React Testing Library).

### 7. Storybook story (optional but recommended)

If Storybook is configured (check `packages/web/` for `.storybook/`), add a story:

```tsx
// StatusBar.stories.tsx
export const Default = { args: { lensMode: 'all', driftCount: 0 } };
export const WithDrift = { args: { lensMode: 'all', driftCount: 5 } };
export const LensStructure = { args: { lensMode: 'structure', driftCount: 0 } };
```

---

## Implementation rules

- **No hex literals in chrome.** Every colour in `StatusBar.tsx`, `LensDropdown.tsx`, and `DriftBadge.tsx` must use `var(--…)` CSS token references. The ESLint `bim-ai/no-hex-in-chrome` rule is active; CI fails on any literal hex in new `.tsx` files. The single exception is the `--color-drift` token initialisation value in the CSS token file itself — token definitions may use `oklch(…)` or `#` values; component code may not.
- **28 px height is fixed.** Do not use `min-height` or `height: auto`. The bar must be exactly 28 px.
- **`--radius-canvas: 0`.** The bar has no border-radius (sharp edges, flush with the canvas bottom).
- **`--ease-paper` for all transitions.** Lens change, drift badge appear/disappear — all use `transition: opacity 240ms var(--ease-paper)`.
- **Lens dispatch reuses T8 commands.** Do not invent a new store or command. Import and use whatever T8 DSC-V3-02 shipped for lens state.
- **No filter dialog.** The lens dropdown is the complete filter surface. No modal, no sheet overlay. This is the A8 antidote — the whole point is that it replaces the dialog.
- **Drift badge opens federation panel.** The onClick for `DriftBadge` must open the federation/links panel. Locate the existing panel toggle action (search `packages/web/src/` for `federationPanel`, `linksPanel`, or `FederationPanel`) and dispatch it.
- **Pydantic alias discipline** does not apply (this is a pure frontend WP), but follow the same `camelCase` prop naming convention in TypeScript interfaces.

---

## Verify gate

```bash
# TypeScript type-check
cd /Users/jhoetter/repos/bim-ai
pnpm tsc --noEmit

# Unit tests
pnpm exec vitest run packages/web/src/chrome/

# ESLint (no-hex-in-chrome)
pnpm exec eslint packages/web/src/chrome/StatusBar.tsx \
                  packages/web/src/chrome/LensDropdown.tsx \
                  packages/web/src/chrome/DriftBadge.tsx

# Format check
pnpm format:check
```

Manual verification (dev server on :3000 or :5173):
- Load a model; status bar appears at 28 px height at canvas bottom.
- `"Show: All"` is visible in slot 5.
- Click slot 5; lens menu opens.
- Select `"Structure"`; canvas ghosts non-structural elements to 25 % in 240 ms.
- Select `"All"`; canvas restores in 240 ms.
- No filter dialog opens at any point.

---

## Commit and push

```bash
git add packages/web/src/chrome/ \
        packages/core/src/index.ts
# Add packages/web/src/design-tokens/*.css if --color-drift was added
git commit -m "$(cat <<'EOF'
feat(chr): CHR-V3-03 status bar with lens dropdown (WP-023)

6-slot 28 px status bar; slot 5 = LensDropdown (A8 antidote, replaces filter dialog).
Lens change dispatches T8 DSC-V3-02 command; ghost opacity 240 ms --ease-paper.
DriftBadge slot 6: hidden at 0, opens federation panel on click, --color-drift token.
No hex literals; --radius-canvas: 0; --text-2xs slots.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
EOF
)"
git push -u origin feat/v3-chr-v3-03-status-bar
```

---

## Final report template

Paste this back to the orchestrator when done:

```
WP: WP-023 / CHR-V3-03
Branch: feat/v3-chr-v3-03-status-bar
Status: done
Tests: <N> passed (StatusBar.test.tsx)
Files changed:
  - packages/web/src/chrome/StatusBar.tsx       (6-slot layout, 28 px)
  - packages/web/src/chrome/LensDropdown.tsx    (slot 5 keystone, --text-sm menu)
  - packages/web/src/chrome/DriftBadge.tsx      (slot 6, --color-drift)
  - packages/core/src/index.ts                  (LensMode type if new)
  - packages/web/src/design-tokens/*.css        (--color-drift if new)
  - <any additional files>
Notes: <renderer ghost-opacity integration deferred if T8 renderer not yet wired; etc.>

Tracker: §2 CHR-V3-03 → change status to `done`
```

330	**UI.** Chip at `--text-sm`, 4 px `--disc-*` stripe at left edge.
331	**Acceptance.** Architekt → Statiker flips stripe, re-orders left-rail stack, preselects status-bar lens to "Structure" in 240 ms. No model mutation.
332	**Effort.** M.
333	
334	#### CHR-V3-03 — Status bar with lens dropdown
335	
336	**Status.** `now`. T8 LNS-V3-01 home.
337	**Scope.** 6-slot status bar; lens dropdown is the keystone (A8 antidote). 28 px, `--color-surface-strong`, `--radius-canvas: 0`.
338	**Data model.** Reuses T8 DSC-V3-02 `defaultLens: LensMode`.
339	**Engine.** Lens dispatch reuses T8 commands. Drift count from `monitorDriftBadge` aggregate.
340	**UI.** `--text-2xs` slots; lens menu `--text-sm`. `--ease-paper` 240 ms. Drift in `--color-drift`.
341	**Acceptance.** "Show: All" → "Show: Structure" ghosts arch to 25 % in 240 ms. No filter dialog. Drift opens federation panel.
342	**Effort.** S.
343	
344	#### CHR-V3-04 — Cmd+K command palette
345	
346	**Status.** `now`. Cross-ref T2 EDT-V3-03 (commands engine; this WP is chrome placement + cross-platform binding).
347	**Scope.** Wire palette to slot 5; OS-aware keybinding; ensure palette is universal escape across all themes.
348	**Data model.** Reuses `cmdPalette/registry.ts`.
349	**Engine.** Reuses fuzzysort + recency slice.
350	**UI.** Centred 480 px modal; canvas dims 40 %; results `--text-sm`. Three categories.
351	**Acceptance.** `Cmd+K` opens anywhere; `"phase"` → top match invokes; `Esc` closes. Cross-platform verified.
352	**Effort.** M.
353	
354	#### CHR-V3-05 — Activity-stream entry + drawer
355	
356	**Status.** `now`. T3 VER-V3-01 cousin.
357	**Scope.** Slot 6 entry + drawer slide-in from right edge. 380 px, layered over right rail (dims 50 %); latest 10 events. Hover preview, click time-travel.
358	**Data model.** Reuses T3 activity-stream schema.
359	**Engine.** Reuses T3 snapshot replay; hover preview to off-screen frame.

file.content:
**UI.** Chip at `--text-sm`, 4 px `--disc-*` stripe at left edge.
**Acceptance.** Architekt → Statiker flips stripe, re-orders left-rail stack, preselects status-bar lens to "Structure" in 240 ms. No model mutation.
**Effort.** M.

#### CHR-V3-03 — Status bar with lens dropdown

**Status.** `now`. T8 LNS-V3-01 home.
**Scope.** 6-slot status bar; lens dropdown is the keystone (A8 antidote). 28 px, `--color-surface-strong`, `--radius-canvas: 0`.
**Data model.** Reuses T8 DSC-V3-02 `defaultLens: LensMode`.
**Engine.** Lens dispatch reuses T8 commands. Drift count from `monitorDriftBadge` aggregate.
**UI.** `--text-2xs` slots; lens menu `--text-sm`. `--ease-paper` 240 ms. Drift in `--color-drift`.
**Acceptance.** "Show: All" → "Show: Structure" ghosts arch to 25 % in 240 ms. No filter dialog. Drift opens federation panel.
**Effort.** S.

#### CHR-V3-04 — Cmd+K command palette

**Status.** `now`. Cross-ref T2 EDT-V3-03 (commands engine; this WP is chrome placement + cross-platform binding).
**Scope.** Wire palette to slot 5; OS-aware keybinding; ensure palette is universal escape across all themes.
**Data model.** Reuses `cmdPalette/registry.ts`.
**Engine.** Reuses fuzzysort + recency slice.
**UI.** Centred 480 px modal; canvas dims 40 %; results `--text-sm`. Three categories.
**Acceptance.** `Cmd+K` opens anywhere; `"phase"` → top match invokes; `Esc` closes. Cross-platform verified.
**Effort.** M.

#### CHR-V3-05 — Activity-stream entry + drawer

**Status.** `now`. T3 VER-V3-01 cousin.
**Scope.** Slot 6 entry + drawer slide-in from right edge. 380 px, layered over right rail (dims 50 %); latest 10 events. Hover preview, click time-travel.
**Data model.** Reuses T3 activity-stream schema.
**Engine.** Reuses T3 snapshot replay; hover preview to off-screen frame.
